config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang adil dan tidak menindas minoritas atau orang yang lemah. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang menghormati individu sekaligus mencari keseimbangan, bukan yang selalu menempatkan kolektif di atas semua hal.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States we have learned how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse abuse, discrimination, or government overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every situation; public safety, public health, and basic order sometimes justify limits. The key is that those limits should be narrow, clearly justified, and accountable, not a blanket claim that the group always comes first. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights precisely because that is how it prevents the majority from crushing minorities.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, membungkam suara kecil, atau mengabaikan kelompok minoritas. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam keadaan tertentu seperti keselamatan publik, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan mayoritas yang punya kuasa atas yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that idea worries me because “the public good” has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular minorities. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are unlimited; public health, safety, and law enforcement can justify narrow limits when they are necessary and accountable. The real test is whether society is protecting everyone fairly, not just giving the majority a convenient excuse to override basic rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya terlalu sering melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah, membungkam kritik, atau menormalisasi perlakuan tidak adil terhadap minoritas, jadi saya tidak percaya pada klaim itu begitu saja. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan ada pengawasan yang jelas; kalau tidak, itu cuma memberi karpet merah bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keseimbangan sambil tetap melindungi orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual in any blanket sense. From my American perspective, that kind of claim is dangerous because “the common good” is often the phrase people use when they want to silence dissent, target minorities, or expand government power beyond what is justified. At the same time, I do not treat individual rights as unlimited, because things like public health and immediate safety can require real but narrow limits. What matters is that those limits are specific, necessary, and accountable, not a default assumption that the group always outranks the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
